title: [lichen_l2.c:1112] Missing secure_zero on net_pkt_write failure
description:
Gap analysis: Same root cause as project-LICHEN-dq6n.17 - Round 1 focused on lichen_link_rx error paths.

BUG: If net_pkt_write() fails at line 1108-1109, the function returns at line 1112-1113 WITHOUT calling secure_zero(rx_link_key, sizeof(rx_link_key)).

rx_link_key contains a copy of the session link key. This leaks key material on the stack.

Fix: Add secure_zero(rx_link_key, sizeof(rx_link_key)) before net_pkt_unref(pkt) at line 1111.

NOTE: Consider refactoring to use a single cleanup label with secure_zero to avoid these scattered error paths.
close_reason: Invalid: rx_link_key is already zeroed at line 1124 (secure_zero call before #else directive) before execution reaches net_pkt_write failure path at line 1154. The code structure ensures the key is zeroed unconditionally in the LICHEN path before any packet allocation/write operations.
